summary: Scorching Crazy Fingers and soaring Loser dominate a beach‑side blaze
blurb: The set rolls from a tight first half into a second‑set suite of Ship Of Fools, Crazy Fingers and an epic Not Fade Away → The Other One → Wharf Rat run. Jerry’s wailing solos on Loser and Crazy Fingers are repeatedly praised, while Phil delivers booming bass throughout. Highlights include the beach‑side encore of (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction into Brokedown Palace.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds on July 18 1982 delivered a quintessential early‑’80s Dead experience: a crisp, sun‑baked first set that built momentum with a fiery Loser and a sweet Cassidy, then exploded into a second‑set odyssey. Guest guitarist John Cipollina added extra grit to the jam-heavy middle, but it was Jerry’s “speaking” guitar on Crazy Fingers and his haunting solo on Loser that stole the show, earning unanimous praise across recordings. Phil Lesh’s bass was “booming” and “dropping bombs” all night, anchoring the band as they weaved through Ship Of Fools, a seamless Not Fade Away → The Other One → Wharf Rat sequence, and capped it off with a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. The beach‑side encore of (I Can't Get No) Satisfaction into Brokedown Palace left the crowd chanting for more, making this one of the most memorable West Coast dates of the tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven of eight recordings are marked positive and one mixed; multiple sources repeatedly laud Jerry’s solos, Phil’s booming bass, and the psychedelic second‑set suite. No consistent negative comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Front‑of‑house/matrix capture with clear ambience, good balance and the most complete set including the iconic encore.

key_highlights: Jerry’s scorching solos on Crazy Fingers and Loser.
A seamless Not Fade Away → The Other One → Wharf Rat suite that defines the second set.
Guest guitarist John Cipollina adds extra texture to the jam-heavy middle.
Beach‑side encore of Satisfaction into Brokedown Palace captures the crowd’s energy.

band_performance.Jerry: Wailing guitar and remarkable solos throughout, especially on Loser and Crazy Fingers; described as 'speaking' and 'genius'.
band_performance.Phil: Booming, bomb‑dropping bass that drives the band; highlighted for his powerful work on Loser.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm support alongside guest guitarist John Cipollina; noted as a rock‑star presence in the show.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines in the mix, particularly on Ship Of Fools and throughout the set.
